title: Particle-Based Export Formula Report
subtitle: Formulas implemented in stress_particle_based.py
output_files: Per-particle values at the selected extract time.
notation: Current sample time returned by EDEM for timestep k.
Interval length from the previous sample to the current sample.
Set of collisions present in the current surfSurf sample, not only newly started collisions.
Number of sampled collisions at timestep k.
Particle index in the cached particle metadata used by the script.
Collision i touches particle p as either first or second particle.
Number of sampled current collisions touching particle p at timestep k.
Mass and radius of particle p from the cached particle metadata.
Collision energies used in the code.
Current force magnitudes used in the code.
Previous-only carry-over collisions stored in state that disappeared before the current sample and are not part of C_k, but still overlap the interval.
Overlap duration of a current collision with the interval [t_(k-1), t_k], not the full contact lifetime.
Carry-over overlap duration attributed to a previous-only tail collision j in R_k.
Host particles selected by the configured host material.

equations.label: P1
equations.title: Interval length and overlap duration
equations.description: The particle report inherits the same interval model as the collision report.

equations.label: P2
equations.title: Current collision count per particle
equations.description: This count uses the current sampled collisions at timestep k, not unique collisions over the entire interval.

equations.label: P3
equations.title: Average current collision-force magnitude per particle
equations.description: If n_p,k is zero, the code writes zero for that particle.

equations.label: P4
equations.title: Energy split from collisions to particles
equations.latex: E_{n,p} = \frac{1}{2}\sum_{i \in C_k,\ i \sim p} E_{n,i},\quad E_{s,p} = \frac{1}{2}\sum_{i \in C_k,\ i \sim p} E_{s,i},\quad E_p = \frac{1}{2}\sum_{i \in C_k,\ i \sim p} E_i
equations.description: Each collision contributes half of its energy to each particle in the pair.

equations.label: P5
equations.title: Particle specific-intensity values
equations.description: If particle mass is zero, the code writes zero.

equations.label: P6
equations.title: Aggregate specific energy by particle
equations.latex: SE_{n,k}^{part} = \frac{\sum_{p \in P} E_{n,p}}{\sum_{p \in P} m_p},\quad SE_{s,k}^{part} = \frac{\sum_{p \in P} E_{s,p}}{\sum_{p \in P} m_p},\quad SE_k^{part} = \frac{\sum_{p \in P} E_p}{\sum_{p \in P} m_p}
equations.description: The implementation aggregates particle energies first, then divides by the sum of valid particle masses. This is not the arithmetic mean of the per-particle SI values.

equations.label: P7
equations.title: Trapezoidal impulse from current matched collisions
equations.description: The same trapezoidal matching rule as the collision report is used for current matched collisions. Only collisions that are present in C_k and matched to previous state contribute to this current term.

equations.label: P8
equations.title: Previous-only tail impulse added only at particle level
equations.description: Equivalent tail terms are also used for tangential and total impulse. R_k is a previous-only carry-over state, not part of the current sampled set C_k. This term is not added to collision-level exports.

equations.label: P9
equations.title: Total particle impulse and accumulated contact time
equations.latex: J_{n,p,k} = J_{n,p,k}^{curr} + \widetilde{J}_{n,p,k}^{tail},\quad T_{p,k}^{contact} = \sum_{i \in C_k,\ i \sim p}\tau_i^{overlap} + \sum_{j \in R_k,\ j \sim p}\tau_j^{tail}
equations.description: The same structure is used for shear and total impulse. Therefore particle impulse and particle contact time may include both current sampled contacts and previous-only tail contributions.

equations.label: P10
equations.title: Particle area and stress values
equations.description: If contact time or particle area is zero, the code writes zero. These exported sigma values are stress-like proxies from the implementation.

equations.label: P11
equations.title: Particle power values
equations.latex: P_{n,p} = \frac{E_{n,p}}{\Delta t_k},\quad P_{s,p} = \frac{E_{s,p}}{\Delta t_k},\quad P_p = \frac{E_p}{\Delta t_k}
equations.description: Power is computed from the current interval length.

equations.label: P12
equations.title: Host-particle rotational metrics
equations.latex: d_{h,k}^{rot} = r_h \|\boldsymbol{\omega}_h\| \Delta t_k,\quad \xi_{h,k} = I[n_{h,k} > 0],\quad D_{h,k}^{rot} = D_{h,k-1}^{rot} + \xi_{h,k} d_{h,k}^{rot},\quad T_{h,k}^{contact} = T_{h,k-1}^{contact} + \xi_{h,k}\Delta t_k
equations.description: The contact indicator is based on the current sampled active collision count for each host particle. It is a current-step gate, not an accumulated overlap measure by itself.

equations.label: P13
equations.title: Collision-side fields still exported in particle_over_time.csv
equations.description: The particle summary CSV also keeps several collision-derived summary columns from the shared timestep core. The f_k^coll term uses the same new-collision subset rule as the collision report, while C_k itself remains the full current sampled collision set.

summary_fields: time
Current sample time t_k.
EDEM time value for the processed timestep.
number_of_collisions
N_k = |C_k|.
Number of sampled collisions in the current surfSurf snapshot.
Ave_number_of_collisions_per_particle
Mean over particles of n_p,k. Eq. P2.
Average number of sampled current collisions per particle.
Ave_contact_duration_per_collision
Mean of d_i over sampled collisions. Eq. P13.
Average full contact duration of current sampled collisions.
total_collision_time
Sum of tau_i^overlap over sampled collisions. Eq. P13.
Total overlap time attributed to the interval.
average_collision_time
Mean of tau_i^overlap over sampled collisions. Eq. P13.
Average overlap time per sampled collision in the current interval.
collision_frequency
N_k^new / Delta t_k. Eq. P13.
Frequency of newly started sampled collisions in the interval.
Ave_TotalColForce_inSys_byParticle
Mean over particles of Fbar_p,k. Eq. P3.
Average current collision-force magnitude per particle, including zero for particles with no current collision.
high_impact_event_fraction
Count(F_i^max > threshold) / N_k using collision max-force magnitude.
Fraction of sampled collisions above the configured impact threshold.
Ave_SI_normal_per_particle
Mean over particles of SI_n,p. Eq. P5.
Average normal specific intensity per particle.
Ave_SI_shear_per_particle
Mean over particles of SI_s,p. Eq. P5.
Average shear specific intensity per particle.
Ave_SI_total_per_particle
Mean over particles of SI_p. Eq. P5.
Average total specific intensity per particle.
Total_normal_specific_energy_by_particle
Aggregate particle normal specific energy. Eq. P6.
Normal energy summed over particles, then divided by summed particle mass.
Total_shear_specific_energy_by_particle
Aggregate particle shear specific energy. Eq. P6.
Shear energy summed over particles, then divided by summed particle mass.
Total_specific_energy_by_particle
Aggregate particle total specific energy. Eq. P6.
Total energy summed over particles, then divided by summed particle mass.
Ave_normal_stress_value_per_particle
Mean over particles of sigma_n,p. Eq. P10.
Average normal stress proxy per particle.
Ave_shear_stress_value_per_particle
Mean over particles of sigma_s,p. Eq. P10.
Average shear stress proxy per particle.
Ave_stress_value_per_particle
Mean over particles of sigma_p. Eq. P10.
Average total stress proxy per particle.
Ave_normal_power_per_particle
Mean over particles of P_n,p. Eq. P11.
Average normal power per particle.
Ave_shear_power_per_particle
Mean over particles of P_s,p. Eq. P11.
Average shear power per particle.
Ave_power_per_particle
Mean over particles of P_p. Eq. P11.
Average total power per particle.
Total_normal_power_by_particle
Sum over particles of P_n,p. Eq. P11.
Total normal power across particles.
Total_shear_power_by_particle
Sum over particles of P_s,p. Eq. P11.
Total shear power across particles.
Total_power_by_particle
Sum over particles of P_p. Eq. P11.
Total power across particles.
Ave_rotational_distance_per_host_particle
Mean of d_h,k^rot over all host particles sampled at k. Eq. P12.
Average host rotational distance in the current interval.
Ave_rotational_distance_total_per_host_particle
Mean of D_h,k^rot over host particles with xi_h,k = 1. Eq. P12.
Average cumulative host rotational distance among currently contacting host particles.
Ave_cumulative_contact_time_per_host_particle
Mean of T_h,k^contact over host particles with xi_h,k = 1. Eq. P12.
Average cumulative host contact time among currently contacting host particles.
extract_fields: Scalar t_k for the extract timestep.
Written once at the top of the extracted block by the CSV expansion helper.
Cached particle ID from the particle metadata.
Particle identifier used by the script.
Cached mass m_p from the particle metadata.
Particle mass used in particle specific-intensity calculations.
Current sampled collision count touching the particle.
Average current collision-force magnitude touching the particle.
Alias of SI_normal_per_particle in the code.
Alias of SI_shear_per_particle in the code.
Alias of SI_total_per_particle in the code.
xi_h,k from Eq. P12 for host particles, else 0.
Current contact indicator written into the particle table.
d_h,k^rot from Eq. P12 for host particles, else 0.
Current rotational distance for host particles.
D_h,k^rot from Eq. P12 for host particles, else 0.
Cumulative rotational distance for host particles while in contact.
notes: All displayed formulas reflect the current implementation in stress_particle_based.py.
Particle-level impulse uses both the matched current trapezoid term and the previous-only tail term. This is a deliberate implementation detail of the current code.
Averages over particles include zero values for particles without collision or without valid denominator in the current interval.
The CSV expansion helper writes scalar fields such as time only once at the top of an extracted block, leaving the remaining rows blank in that scalar column.
If a denominator is zero, if no valid term exists, or if a feature flag is disabled in Stress_settings.txt, the corresponding exported values remain 0.
